name: General Tso's Chicken III
recipeIngredient: 1 pound chicken thigh or breast, boned, skinned, cut into 2-inch pieces
4 green onions cut diagonally into 1-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
The dish is not difficult to make and can be prepared partially in advance. The crunch texture of the chicken pieces is achieved by coating them with egg and cornstarch, then deep-frying them twice: once to cook through and once at a higher temperature to make them brown and crisp. The velvety sweet-sour sauce, combined with the chicken at the last minute, provides a wonderful counterpoint. Adjust the amount of heat as desired; 16 small dried red peppers provide a moderate amount of heat.
1. Whisk together thoroughly the egg and cornstarch. Add pieces of chicken, turning to coat evenly.
2. Heat vegetable oil in a wok or deep-fat fryer to 350°F (175°C). Fry chicken, a few pieces at a time until lightly browned and just cooked through. Drain on paper towels.
3. Combine sauce ingredients; mix well. Set aside. The chicken can be fried the first time up to one hour in advance. The sauce can be combined several days in advance and kept covered in the refrigerator.
4. In a frying pan, heat 1 tablespoon oil until hot. Add chili peppers and cook until blackened. Add onions and stir-fry about one minute. Add garlic and ginger, cooking briefly but do not brown. Remove from heat.
5. Reheat oil to 400°F (200°C). Return chicken to fat in batches and fry until crisp and golden brown. Drain on paper towels.
6. Re-stir the sauce and add it to the frying pan with onions and peppers. Cook, stirring until thickened and bubbly. Add chicken and cook, stirring, until well coated and heated through.
7. Serve over rice.
